div1.entryFree: Ballad, subst., popular song: is there not a b. of the king and the beggar? LLL I, 2, 114. LLL I, 2, 114 All's I, 3, 64. II, 1, 175. Wint. IV, 4, 186. Wint. IV, 4, 186 Wint. IV, 4, 186 Wint. IV, 4, 186 610 etc. H4A II, 2, 48. H4B IV, 3, 52. H5 V, 2, 167 (mentioned with contempt: a rhyme is but a b.). Song, poem in general: I will get Peter Quince to write a b. of this dream, Mids. IV, 1, 221. a woeful b. made to his mistress' eyebrow, As II, 7, 148.
